mutation: composite: goal generalized from a single-shot cutting-stock cover to a MIN-cost SEQUENCE of setups, with output constrained by a bounded knife-motion (reconfiguration-distance) chain between consecutive setups
theme: Slitting coils when knives crawl, not jump
title: Slitting Coils When Knives Crawl, Not Jump
strategies: one-unit-at-a-time input-order baseline: never batch a pattern's full capacity, never reorder -- just visit each demand type once in the order given and repeat single-unit runs (trivial)
value-greedy cutting-stock: batch each pattern to its full useful capacity, but always chase the single highest-value pattern next, ignoring how far the knives must travel to reach it (greedy)

value-per-hop reachability planning: score every still-useful pattern by value divided by (1 + knife-motion hops from the current position), turning pattern selection into an implicit shortest path through pattern space that clusters nearby visits before paying to cross a gap (strong)

full joint optimization (headroom above strong): design patterns that intentionally combine MULTIPLE different demand widths per setup (not just one width repeated) and jointly plan pattern composition + order + run length via a real shortest-path/DP over the reachability graph
